sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presented with a 5-day history of right knee pain following a skiing accident where he twisted his knee while landing a jump on his boat.
The pain has been constant and worsened with squatting and bending.
The patient reports swelling and pain in the knee, but denies hearing or feeling anything pop during the accident.
He has not experienced any numbness or tingling in his foot.
The patient has been taking over-the-counter Aleve twice a day and applying a cold pack to the affected area, which has provided some relief.
He has a history of tonsillectomy.
Past medical history: The patient has hypertension, for which he is taking lisinopril 20mg daily.
His blood pressure has been well-controlled, with readings of 125/80 at home and 120/77 in the office today.
The patient denies any other medical history or allergies.
Review of systems: The patient denies any symptoms in other organs.
Current medications: Lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 120/77, pulse rate is not mentioned, oxygen saturation is not mentioned, and temperature is not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient's neck exam is unremarkable, with no lymphadenopathy.
The heart exam is normal, with a regular rate and rhythm and no murmur.
The lung exam is clear to auscultation bilaterally.
The musculoskeletal exam reveals ecchymosis, edema, and an effusion on the right knee.
The patient has pain to palpation of the right medial knee and decreased range of motion.
The varus and valgus tests are negative, and the Lachman sign is also negative.
The patient has a palpable dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulse, with no lower extremity edema.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a medial collateral ligament strain in the right knee, and an effusion with inflammation.
Problem list: Medial collateral ligament strain; right knee effusion; hypertension; past medical history of tonsillectomy.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg daily for pain management and to reduce inflammation.
I ordered an x-ray of the right knee to rule out any broken bones.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee and prevent future injuries.
I will continue the patient on lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension and ordered a lipid panel to monitor lipid levels.
I administered a tetanus shot for health maintenance.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen again after the results of the x-ray are available, and the patient will rest the knee to avoid further injury.
